name: Raisin Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. Grease a cookie sheet.
3. In a large bowl, combine softened butter and sugar. Add 2 eggs plus 1 egg yolk (reserve remaining egg white) and beat until light and fluffy. Stir in anise seeds, lemon peel, and orange peel.
4. In a separate bowl, mix flour, baking powder, and baking soda. Stir into the butter mixture until combined.
5. Stir in chopped walnuts and raisins. Divide the dough into two equal parts.
6. Shape each part into a 12-inch roll on the greased cookie sheet, leaving space between for spreading.
7. Brush tops of rolls with slightly beaten egg white.
8. Bake at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 30 minutes in the upper third of the oven until lightly browned. Remove from oven.
9. Reduce temperature to 325 degrees Fahrenheit.
10. Using a sharp knife, cut each roll into 1/2-inch slices diagonally. Place slices, cut side down, on the cookie sheet.
11. Return to oven and bake at 325 degrees Fahrenheit for an additional 10 to 12 minutes or until nearly dry. Cool on wire racks. Makes about 4 dozen cookies.
Biscotti are best enjoyed when dunked in coffee. If eaten dry, they may be difficult to consume, but they are heavenly dipped in coffee. They can also be dipped in chocolate for an elegant presentation (dip them halfway up).
